You are working on the AeterOS project. I am now authorizing you to perform a full aggressive cleanup and refactor across the entire workspace.

Your goal is to eliminate ALL warnings, even if this requires architectural changes, deep refactors, or redesigning unsafe patterns.

You are allowed to modify ANY part of the codebase, including:
- kernel code
- allocators
- static mutable memory patterns
- V-Node heaps
- runtime loops
- IPC structures
- system services
- scheduler logic
- memory management
- unsafe blocks
- module APIs
- crate boundaries
- architectural patterns

You may:
- replace `static mut` with safer patterns (Mutex, spinlocks, OnceCell, lazy_static, custom primitives)
- redesign allocator initialization
- rewrite V-Node runtime loops
- restructure modules
- remove or rewrite dead code
- change function signatures
- reorganize crates
- introduce new abstractions
- apply Clippy suggestions even if they require logic changes
- migrate code to Rust 2024 idioms
- rewrite unsafe code into safe equivalents where possible
- redesign IPC or memory models if needed

You must:
- ensure the system still compiles
- ensure the system still boots (as far as possible)
- ensure the refactor is consistent and complete
- produce a patch (diff) with all changes

Steps:

1. Install Clippy:
   rustup component add clippy

2. Run deep analysis:
   cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -W clippy::all -W clippy::pedantic

3. Apply ALL fixes required to eliminate warnings, even if they require architectural changes.

4. After completing the refactor, generate a full patch (diff) that I can apply.

5. If a subsystem must be redesigned to remove warnings, redesign it.

Goal: Zero warnings. Maximum code quality. Full modernization. No restrictions.

Begin now.
